planning_note: DAG-validated acyclic: roots {065, 068}; 065 -> 066 -> 067; 069 dep {065, 066, 067, 068}. Realizes NIP-0002 Stage 1 (consumer-readiness, the prerequisite for every larger project count) + completes the 1-brownfield point of the 0-n spectrum, then re-pilots to PROVE it. Features 065-068 are framework-internal (tool + bootstrap code + protocol/doc updates) and executable without any external repo; feature 069 re-pilots against a scratch/throwaway consumer (operator option c, as in phase 007) and needs no external repo access. PROPOSED, not activated — no feature enters contract negotiation before gate H-PHASE-008. NIP-0002 Stages 2-4 (0-case greenfield genesis NDEBT-030, n-case multi-repo tooling + membership registry NDEBT-031, 04/05 coordination protocols) are OUT OF SCOPE, deferred to phase 009 and evidence-gated on feature 069's re-pilot. CLOSED 2026-07-21 (phase 008 complete): all features 065-069 complete. 065/066 (ADR-004) + 067 (SHA pin) landed; 068 resolved-by-design (Option A, no code); 069 re-pilot proved findings A/B resolved against a real bootstrapped consumer. Per-feature ledger in run_state.

features.name: Governance-root resolution (ADR-004 decision 1)
features.description: Give tools/ecosystem_preflight.py a governance-root distinct from the repository root so it locates the injected .nizam/ payload instead of assuming the framework-root layout (ADR-004 decision 1; NDEBT-027). Add a --governance-root option; default behaviour discovers the injected payload directory (conventionally .nizam/) under --repo-root; --self-fixture keeps governance-root == repo-root unchanged. Resolve REQUIRED_REFERENCE_PATHS against the governance-root (collect_required_reference_findings), and treat the injected payload directory as expected rather than an untracked blocking finding (the clean-state check). Mirror ecosystem_preflight.py's documented exit-code table + stdlib-only style. Add fixtures_self_test.sh probes and e2e_bootstrap_test.sh coverage proving a real bootstrapped consumer yields a clean Preflight, and that --self-fixture is unchanged.
acceptance_tests: ecosystem_preflight.py accepts a --governance-root option and, given a real bootstrapped consumer (payload under .nizam/), resolves its required schema references and does NOT emit the injected .nizam/ as an untracked blocking finding.

Without --governance-root the tool discovers the injected payload directory under --repo-root; with --self-fixture the governance-root equals the repo-root and behaviour is byte-for-byte unchanged (regression-guarded).
A clean Preflight against a real bootstrapped scratch consumer returns PASS (or an operator-approved PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS), not the phase-007 hard FAIL.
fixtures_self_test.sh and e2e_bootstrap_test.sh gain covering cases; bash tools/validate.sh is green 15/15.

features.name: Provenance-pin anchoring for the baseline (ADR-004 decision 2)
features.description: Make build_baseline_document anchor framework_references to the injected framework pin recorded in the governance-root's provenance.json (framework_version / tag), not to resolve_head_revision(repo_root) (ADR-004 decision 2; NDEBT-028). repository_references continues to anchor to the consumer's HEAD, so the two reference categories record two distinct correct facts (which framework vs which consumer revision). Preserve the --self-fixture degenerate case (governance-root == repo-root) where the two coincide. Add a self-test probe asserting a bootstrapped consumer's baseline names the injected tag, not the consumer HEAD.
acceptance_tests: build_baseline_document reads the governance-root provenance.json and sets framework_references[].revision to the recorded framework pin (tag/framework_version), not the consumer's HEAD.
repository_references still records the consumer's HEAD revision; the emitted baseline remains schema-valid against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json.
Under --self-fixture the behaviour is unchanged (framework and repo revisions legitimately coincide); a self-test probe covers both the real-consumer and self-fixture cases.

features.name: Bootstrap commit-SHA pinning (NDEBT-033)
features.description: Harden bootstrap provenance so a moved release tag cannot replay a different payload under the same pin (NDEBT-033). bootstrap.sh resolves the selected release tag to its commit SHA at clone time, persists BOTH the tag and the resolved SHA in provenance.json, and check_provenance_tag / --verify-only compares the recorded SHA as well as the tag, rejecting a tag that now resolves to a different commit, while preserving the existing immutable-ref (floating-ref refusal) validation. Update the provenance-shape documentation in lockstep: ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md Section 7 and standard/GIP.md Section 4. Extend e2e_bootstrap_test.sh to assert the recorded SHA and that verify-only fails on a SHA mismatch. Coordinated with feature 066 because both touch the provenance contract.
acceptance_tests: bootstrap.sh records both tag and the resolved commit SHA in provenance.json; --verify-only PASSES only when both match and FAILS on a SHA mismatch even if the tag string matches.
The floating-ref refusal (pinned-immutable-tag precondition) is preserved unchanged.
ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md Section 7 and standard/GIP.md Section 4 describe the tag+SHA provenance shape consistently (C10 green).
tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh gains the SHA-record and SHA-mismatch cases; bash tools/validate.sh green 15/15.

features.name: Brownfield bootstrap reconciliation (GIP Sec 5.1; NDEBT-032)
features.description: Mechanize GIP Section 5.1 rename-and-diff in bootstrap.sh so a consumer with a pre-existing root-level CONTEXT.md / AGENTS.md / CI configuration is never silently overwritten (NDEBT-032; the ex-conditional phase-007 feature 062). Today bootstrap.sh performs an atomic replace of the dedicated .nizam/ target only; extend it to detect a colliding pre-existing root-level file and preserve it under a renamed path (e.g. .pre-nizam) for hand reconciliation, and add the corresponding e2e coverage. Completes the 1-brownfield point of the 0-n spectrum (ecosystem/00 Section 3).
acceptance_tests: File preservation (distinct from CI merging): a pre-existing root-level file that collides with an injected artifact (CONTEXT.md, AGENTS.md) remains intact on disk AND is copied to a deterministic, collision-safe renamed destination (a stable convention per GIP Section 5.1, e.g. a `.pre-nizam`-suffixed sibling) for hand reconciliation; bootstrap.sh never overwrites the original in place.

CI configuration merging (distinct from file preservation): a consumer's own CI configuration is added to, never replaced — the consumer's existing CI files are left in place and the injected CI is added alongside.

The exact set of colliding root-level paths handled and the resulting post-bootstrap files (original + renamed copy for preserved files; original + added for CI) are enumerated in the feature's contract and asserted by tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh against a non-empty (brownfield) target.
The existing empty/greenfield atomic-inject path (no collisions) is unchanged (regression-guarded); bash tools/validate.sh green 15/15.
features.resolution: RESOLVED-BY-DESIGN 2026-07-21 (operator Option A). The detect-before-fix pass found the acceptance criteria below rest on a false premise: GIP Section 5.1 point 3 states bootstrap.sh injects ONLY the .nizam/ payload and never writes to a consumer root CONTEXT.md/AGENTS.md/CI, so it cannot silently overwrite them (coexistence safe by construction), and a bootstrap.sh root-file rename-and-diff would CONTRADICT that protocol. No bootstrap.sh mechanization was built (anti-hallucination: no fabricated code); instead ecosystem/00 Sec 3+5.1 (v0.4.0) were corrected and NDEBT-032 moved to Resolved. The acceptance_tests above are retained as the original proposal-time contract, superseded by this resolution.

features.name: Re-pilot the fixed single-project loop, prove it, prioritise phase 009 + phase close
features.description: Re-run the full ecosystem loop against a real, freshly bootstrapped scratch/throwaway consumer (operator option c, as in phase 007) using the FIXED tools (065-068): confirm a clean Preflight PASS with no hand-applied workaround, a baseline whose framework_references correctly names the injected pin, and SHA-verified provenance. Record the H-CONSUMER-UPGRADE gate exercise before the bootstrap (NDEBT-018). Capture any residual friction as new NDEBT-* rows with evidence under .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/. Then REFINE AND VALIDATE the already-authored phase-009 candidate scope in docs/planning/ROADMAP.md (the 'Deferred -> Phase-009 candidate scope' subsection this phase-008 proposal wrote) against the re-pilot evidence — confirming or re-ordering NIP-0002 Stages 2-4 (0-case greenfield genesis, n-case multi-repo tooling + required membership registry, 04/05 coordination protocols), not authoring it from scratch. Close phase 008: run_state feature_complete (065-069) + phase_complete; manifest phase-008 -> complete; per-feature scope ledger.
acceptance_tests: The fixed loop runs Preflight -> Baseline -> Audit -> Compare against a real bootstrapped scratch consumer producing schema-valid artifacts, with a clean Preflight PASS and a correctly provenance-anchored baseline and NO hand-applied workaround (the phase-007 findings A/B are demonstrably resolved).
The H-CONSUMER-UPGRADE re-pilot adoption is recorded in run_state before the bootstrap runs; any residual friction is an NDEBT-* row with externalised evidence.

docs/planning/ROADMAP.md's existing phase-009 candidate scope (NIP-0002 Stages 2-4) is refined/validated against the re-pilot evidence (confirmed or re-ordered), not re-authored; run_state and manifest reflect phase-008 completion; bash tools/validate.sh green 15/15.
